# UC-22.11.71 · Security Event Correlation — Payment API Errors with Concurrent Admin Logons

## Description

Correlates spikes in payment API `5xx` responses with privileged session starts in the same window so automated reviews flag potential tampering or misconfiguration during maintenance abuse.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app, windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, payment:api:json. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Align `span` to traffic profile. (2) Maintain privileged user list from IAM. (3) Tune err5xx threshold per service. (4) Store notable-worthy rows in summary index. Indexes required: `index=app`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=payment:api:json`; Lookups: `pci_privileged_users`, `pci_privileged_users.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` `payment:api:json`; `index=windows` EventCode 4624 LogonType 10.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `span` to traffic profile. (2) Maintain privileged user list from IAM. (3) Tune err5xx threshold per service. (4) Store notable-worthy rows in summary index.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype="payment:api:json" status>=500 earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as err5xx by _time, host
| where err5xx>100
| join type=inner _time [
    search index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-4h
    | lookup pci_privileged_users.csv TargetUserName OUTPUT priv
    | where priv="true"
    | bin _time span=5m
    | stats dc(TargetUserName) as priv_logons by _time
  ]
| table _time, host, err5xx, priv_logons
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Event Correlation — Payment API Errors with Concurrent Admin Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 10.2.2)** — Correlates spikes in payment API `5xx` responses with privileged session starts in the same window so automated reviews flag potential tampering or misconfiguration during maintenance abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `payment:api:json`; `index=windows` EventCode 4624 LogonType 10. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: payment:api:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype="payment:api:json", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **_time, host**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where err5xx>100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.dest span=5m | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Security Event Correlation — Payment API Errors with Concurrent Admin Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 10.2.2)** — Correlates spikes in payment API `5xx` responses with privileged session starts in the same window so automated reviews flag potential tampering or misconfiguration during maintenance abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `payment:api:json`; `index=windows` EventCode 4624 LogonType 10. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, err5xx, host, priv_logons.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (err5xx vs priv_logons), dual-axis line, table.

## SPL

```spl
index=app sourcetype="payment:api:json" status>=500 earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as err5xx by _time, host
| where err5xx>100
| join type=inner _time [
    search index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-4h
    | lookup pci_privileged_users.csv TargetUserName OUTPUT priv
    | where priv="true"
    | bin _time span=5m
    | stats dc(TargetUserName) as priv_logons by _time
  ]
| table _time, host, err5xx, priv_logons
| sort _time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Timeline (err5xx vs priv_logons), dual-axis line, table.
